claimText: Direct taxes are progressive in nature and their incidence and impact fall on the same person, meaning the tax burden cannot be shifted.
evidenceExcerpt: Direct taxes (e.g., Income, Corporate, Capital Gains) are progressive... and their incidence and impact fall on the same person. The burden of direct taxes cannot be shifted
examUse: Statement-based questions comparing the nature and incidence of direct vs. indirect taxes.
distractorHooks: Stating the burden of direct taxes can be shifted to consumers

claimText: Indirect taxes are regressive in nature, applying the same rate regardless of income, and their burden is shifted to the final consumer.
evidenceExcerpt: Indirect taxes (e.g., GST, Customs, Excise) are regressive... and their burden is shifted to the consumer. Indirect taxes are regressive, applying the same rate regardless of income.
examUse: Statement-based questions on the economic impact and burden-shifting of indirect taxes.
distractorHooks: Stating the incidence and impact of indirect taxes fall on the same person

claimText: Direct taxes are administered by the Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT), whereas indirect taxes are administered by the Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC).
evidenceExcerpt: Direct taxes are administered by the CBDT, while indirect taxes are administered by the CBIC.
examUse: Correct-pair or institution-feature questions matching tax types to their respective administrative bodies.
distractorHooks: Claiming a single unified board administers both direct and indirect taxes

claimText: Excise duty on petroleum and liquor remains outside the Goods and Services Tax (GST) framework.
evidenceExcerpt: Excise duty on petroleum and liquor remains outside the GST framework.
examUse: Statement-based questions on the scope and exemptions of the GST framework.
distractorHooks: Claiming petroleum and liquor are fully integrated into the GST framework
Claiming only liquor is outside GST while petroleum is included
